questions.prompt: What should AFK do by default when a setup command needs agent targets?
questions.description: This decides whether detection replaces repeated agent prompts or only narrows their options.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Auto-detect installed compatible agent surfaces and use those detected targets by default, showing the detected targets once in the setup summary.

questions.prompt: How much interactive confirmation should remain after detection?
questions.description: The goal is to reduce repeated prompts while keeping setup behavior understandable.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Do not ask a separate agent-selection question in the normal path. Show detected targets in the final plan/summary and keep explicit CLI overrides such as --agent for manual control.

questions.prompt: Which setup surfaces should use detection for target selection?
questions.description: Rules, MCPs, hooks, utilities, and skill-specific installs have slightly different target models.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use detection for rules, MCPs, hooks, and utilities. Keep universal skills always installed to .agents/skills, and use detection only for additional agent-specific skill providers.

questions.prompt: What should happen when no compatible agent target is detected for a selected setup area?
questions.description: This affects first-time users, machines with unusual config paths, and noninteractive setup.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Skip that target-dependent area with a clear note explaining that no compatible agent config was detected, while still allowing explicit --agent overrides.

questions.prompt: What should count as an installed agent surface?
questions.description: A broad directory check is lower friction but may hit stale residue; a config-file check is safer but may miss valid installs.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use conservative config-file or known directory checks per agent, and keep the detection rules centralized so each supported agent has explicit evidence paths.

questions.prompt: What should success look like for this change?
questions.description: This will drive facts and verification later.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Setup no longer asks repeated agent target questions in the default interactive flow; dry-run output shows detected targets; --agent still narrows or overrides targets; tests cover detected, missing, and explicit-target paths.
